summary: Collapse identical mechanism table into a note; render lite_vs_full, per_tool_counts, and never_called in effect decomposition section
context: All 8 mechanisms showed identical associated difference (-3.19) because the same conditions use all Twining tools, making the heavy-user/non-user split identical. The useful data (lite_vs_full comparison, per-tool utilization, never-called tools) was computed by Python but never rendered in the markdown report.
rationale: When all mechanism diffs are identical the table is actively misleading — it implies differentiation that does not exist. Collapsing to a single explanatory note and surfacing the actually-informative sub-sections (lite_vs_full delta, per-tool counts, never-called tools) gives researchers the correct picture without noise.

alternatives.option: Keep existing table and add sub-sections below it
alternatives.reason_rejected: A table with 8 rows all showing the same value is misleading and wastes space. The note is more honest about what the data shows.
